description: Canonical parameter dictionary for bipolar junction transistors, synthesized from a real-datasheet corpus (onsemi, Nexperia, Infineon, NXP, ST, Diotec, Central Semiconductor, MCC) spanning six sub-shapes that share one measurement envelope: (1) small-signal / general-purpose (2N3904/06, BC547/57, 2N2222A); (2) power (TIP31C/32C, MJE3055/2955T, 2N3055, D44H11); (3) Darlington (TIP120) which additionally populate internal_resistor_1/2 and clamp_diode_forward_voltage; (4) RF / high-frequency (2N5109, BFR93A, BFU550) which populate transition/maximum-oscillation frequency, the RF gain family (power_gain_insertion/maximum, associated_gain), noise_figure, and output_power_1db_compression/third_order_intercept; (5) fast switching (2N2369A) which populate the switching-time keys; and (6) digital / bias-resistor transistors (DTC114E) which populate internal_resistor_1/2/ratio and on/off_state_input_voltage. A part's sub_type selects which optional keys apply; the breakdown voltages, dc_current_gain, saturation voltages, cutoff currents, capacitances, power/thermal, and temperature keys are common. component.polarity carries the transistor type: NPN is 'positive', PNP is 'negative' - store magnitudes, never let a datasheet's leading minus signs on a PNP leak into the numbers. A breakdown voltage that appears both as an absolute-maximum rating (VCEO) and as a characterized value at a test current (BVCEO at IC) is ONE key distinguished by limitClass, not two containers. A gain bin (BC547A/B/C) is one grade per document. dc_current_gain (hFE) and every condition-swept spec is array:true - never collapse the I_C sweep into one row. Condition axes for this family: V_CE, V_CB, V_EB (junction bias voltages), I_C, I_B, I_E (terminal currents), F (frequency, for fT/gain/noise/S-parameter spot values), R_BE (base-emitter termination), R_S/R_G (noise source resistance), T_J, T_A, T_C, T_SP. Pin functions are BASE, COLLECTOR, EMITTER (the tab/case of a TO-220/TO-3 power part is the COLLECTOR; a digital transistor's input/output pins are electrically BASE-via-R1 / COLLECTOR - keep the electrical identity and model the network as internal_resistor keys). Base-SI throughout; the dimensionless current gain hFE and the resistor ratio use V/V; RF output power printed in dBm converts to W (P[W]=10^((dBm-30)/10)) with the dBm figure kept in conditionsVerbatim; small-signal h-parameters use ohm (hie), S (hoe), and V/V (hfe, hre). Safe-operating-area and full S-parameter matrices are graph/file-only and out of scope; capture the tabulated scalar figures. Aliases normalize the printed datasheet symbols.

parameters.name: Collector-emitter breakdown / sustaining voltage
parameters.hint: Collector-emitter voltage with the base open. The same key carries the absolute_max rating (VCEO) and the characterized breakdown (BVCEO at a stated I_C, I_B=0), distinguished by limitClass. On a PNP store the magnitude.

parameters.name: Collector-emitter breakdown, base terminated
parameters.hint: Collector-emitter breakdown with the base-emitter shorted (VCES) or resistor-terminated (VCER at a stated R_BE) - higher than VCEO. Absolute_max rating or characterized value, per limitClass.

parameters.name: Collector-base breakdown voltage
parameters.hint: Collector-base voltage with the emitter open. Absolute_max (VCBO) and/or characterized (BVCBO at a small I_C, I_E=0), per limitClass.

parameters.name: Emitter-base breakdown voltage
parameters.hint: Emitter-base voltage with the collector open - typically low (4-7 V); exceeding it degrades hFE. Absolute_max (VEBO) and/or characterized (BVEBO at a small I_E), per limitClass.

parameters.name: Continuous collector current
parameters.hint: Maximum continuous (DC) collector current (absolute_max). On a digital transistor this is also the output current IO.

parameters.name: Peak collector current
parameters.hint: Maximum peak/pulsed collector current at a stated pulse width (absolute_max).

parameters.name: Base current
parameters.hint: Maximum continuous base current (absolute_max).

parameters.name: Peak base current
parameters.hint: Maximum peak/pulsed base current (absolute_max).

parameters.name: Peak emitter current
parameters.hint: Maximum peak emitter current (absolute_max).

parameters.name: Input voltage (digital transistor)
parameters.hint: Digital (bias-resistor) transistor: the maximum input voltage (into the series R1), often asymmetric positive/negative (absolute_max).

parameters.name: DC current gain (hFE)
parameters.hint: DC current gain, dimensionless (unit V/V), swept over I_C at a fixed V_CE (array over I_C, one measurement per point; hold V_CE in the conditions). A Darlington reaches ~1000; a digital transistor's GI is the DC current transfer ratio. The min/max spread at a point is what a gain bin buys.

parameters.name: Collector-emitter saturation voltage
parameters.hint: Collector-emitter saturation voltage at a stated collector current and forcing base current (array over I_C, with I_B in the conditions). A Darlington's is higher (extra junction). One measurement per (I_C, I_B) point.

parameters.name: Base-emitter saturation voltage
parameters.hint: Base-emitter voltage in saturation at a stated I_C and forcing I_B (array over I_C).

parameters.name: Base-emitter on voltage
parameters.hint: Base-emitter forward voltage in the active region at a stated I_C and V_CE (array over I_C). A Darlington's is ~2x (two stacked junctions).

parameters.name: Collector-base cutoff current
parameters.hint: Collector-base leakage with the emitter open, at a stated V_CB (array over V_CB, and T_J - rises with temperature).

parameters.name: Collector-emitter cutoff current
parameters.hint: Collector-emitter leakage at a stated V_CE, with the base open (ICEO), shorted (ICES), or reverse-biased (ICEX/ICEV) - note the base condition. Array over V_CE and T_J.

parameters.name: Emitter-base cutoff current
parameters.hint: Emitter-base leakage with the collector open, at a stated V_EB.

parameters.name: Base cutoff current
parameters.hint: Base-terminal leakage with the collector-emitter biased and the base-emitter reverse-biased (V_CE, V_EB(off) in the conditions) - a distinct OFF-characteristic row from the collector cutoff on some small-signal parts.

parameters.name: Transition frequency (gain-bandwidth product)
parameters.hint: The frequency at which the small-signal current gain falls to unity, at a stated I_C and V_CE (array over I_C, measured at a high test frequency). Spans MHz (jellybean) to tens of GHz (microwave).

parameters.name: Maximum oscillation frequency
parameters.hint: RF: the frequency at which the maximum available power gain falls to unity. Often graph-derived rather than tabulated.

parameters.name: Output (collector-base) capacitance
parameters.hint: Common-base output (collector-base) capacitance at a stated V_CB (array over V_CB) and test frequency, emitter open. The dominant Miller-feedback capacitance.

parameters.name: Input (emitter-base) capacitance
parameters.hint: Common-base input (emitter-base) capacitance at a stated V_EB and test frequency, collector open.

parameters.name: Feedback (reverse) capacitance
parameters.hint: RF: the reverse (feedback) capacitance at a stated bias - sets the internal feedback that limits stable gain.

parameters.name: Collector-emitter capacitance
parameters.hint: RF: collector-emitter capacitance at a stated V_CE and frequency.

parameters.name: Turn-on delay time
parameters.hint: Switching: delay from the input edge to the start of collector-current rise, at stated drive currents.

parameters.name: Rise time
parameters.hint: Switching: collector-current 10-90% rise time.

parameters.name: Storage time
parameters.hint: Switching: delay from turn-off drive to the start of collector-current fall (stored-charge removal); dominates a saturated BJT's turn-off.

parameters.name: Fall time
parameters.hint: Switching: collector-current 90-10% fall time.

parameters.name: Turn-on time
parameters.hint: Switching: aggregate turn-on time (delay + rise) when the components are not tabulated separately.

parameters.name: Turn-off time
parameters.hint: Switching: aggregate turn-off time (storage + fall).

parameters.name: Small-signal current gain (hfe)
parameters.hint: Small-signal forward current gain (the AC h-parameter hfe) at a stated V_CE, I_C, and frequency (dimensionless, V/V). Note: a bare 'hfe' in extracted text is case-ambiguous with the DC hFE (dc_current_gain), which owns that token; this key is the small-signal AC parameter.

parameters.name: Small-signal input impedance (hie)
parameters.hint: Common-emitter small-signal input impedance at a stated bias and frequency.

parameters.name: Small-signal output admittance (hoe)
parameters.hint: Common-emitter small-signal output admittance (siemens) at a stated bias and frequency.

parameters.name: Reverse voltage transfer ratio (hre)
parameters.hint: Common-emitter reverse voltage transfer ratio (dimensionless, V/V).

parameters.name: Noise figure
parameters.hint: Noise figure at a stated bias, source resistance, and frequency (array over F and/or I_C). NFmin is the minimum over source matching.

parameters.name: Insertion power gain
parameters.hint: RF: insertion / common-emitter power gain (|S21e|^2) at a stated frequency and bias (array over F, I_C). A tabulated spot figure; the full S-matrix is out of scope.

parameters.name: Maximum available / stable power gain
parameters.hint: RF: maximum available (Gma/MAG) or maximum stable (Gms/MSG) power gain at a stated frequency and bias (array over F, I_C).

parameters.name: Associated gain (at minimum noise figure)
parameters.hint: RF: the power gain obtained under the source match that yields NFmin, at a stated frequency and bias.

parameters.name: Output power at 1 dB gain compression
parameters.hint: RF: output power at 1 dB gain compression, at a stated frequency and bias. Convert a printed dBm to W (P=10^((dBm-30)/10)); keep the dBm figure in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Output third-order intercept (OIP3)
parameters.hint: RF: output-referred third-order intercept point, at a stated frequency and bias. Convert a printed dBm to W; keep the dBm figure in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Maximum input power (RF)
parameters.hint: RF: maximum RF input power (absolute_max). Convert a printed dBm to W; keep the dBm figure in conditionsVerbatim.

parameters.name: Internal resistor R1
parameters.hint: Integrated resistor R1: on a digital (bias-resistor) transistor the input series resistor from the INPUT pin to the intrinsic base; on a Darlington a base-emitter bleeder. No SI prefix - 10 kohm is 10000.

parameters.name: Internal resistor R2
parameters.hint: Integrated resistor R2: the base-emitter shunt on a digital transistor, or a base-emitter bleeder on a Darlington.

parameters.name: Internal resistor ratio (R2/R1)
parameters.hint: Digital transistor: the dimensionless ratio of the two integrated resistors (sets the input-threshold behavior).

parameters.name: Integral clamp-diode forward voltage
parameters.hint: Darlington: the forward voltage of the integral collector-emitter freewheeling/clamp diode at a stated current (often only shown in the equivalent circuit - capture a tabulated value only).

parameters.name: On-state input voltage threshold
parameters.hint: Digital transistor: the input voltage that turns the output on to a stated collector current at a low V_CE.

parameters.name: Off-state input voltage threshold
parameters.hint: Digital transistor: the input voltage below which the output is off (leakage-level collector current at a stated V_CE).

parameters.name: Second-breakdown collector current
parameters.hint: Power BJT: the forward-bias second-breakdown collector-current limit at a stated V_CE and time - the one tabular point of the otherwise graph-only safe operating area.

parameters.name: Total power dissipation
parameters.hint: Maximum total power dissipation, rated at a reference temperature (case T_C=25 C for a heatsinked power part, ambient T_A=25 C for free air). Emit one measurement per reference temperature - a consumer must not apply the case-cooled figure in free air.

parameters.name: Junction-to-case thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Junction-to-case (or junction-to-mounting-base) thermal resistance (power parts).

parameters.name: Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Junction-to-ambient thermal resistance (K/W and degC/W are identical; normalize to degC/W).

parameters.name: Junction-to-solder-point thermal resistance
parameters.hint: Junction-to-solder-point thermal resistance (SMD parts characterized to the PCB pad).

parameters.name: Junction temperature (maximum)
parameters.hint: Maximum operating junction temperature (absolute_max).

parameters.name: Operating temperature range
parameters.hint: The operating (junction or ambient) temperature range (min/max).

parameters.name: Storage temperature range
parameters.hint: Absolute-maximum storage temperature range (min/max).
